item.text: I’ll query Haderach first, then verify its guidance against the checkout before touching code. After that I’ll isolate the allocator state transition, add a regression test that visibly requires backtracking, patch the general state handling, and run the pinned container test.

arguments.task: Dynamic Resource Allocation structured allocator rejects valid allocation with list-valued device attributes when solving requires backtracking; reproduce, identify incorrect state handling, implement general fix, add visible regression coverage, run focused allocator tests.

item.text: Haderach points to branch-local constraint state: a list-set intersection is narrowed on `add`, but allegedly not restored on the matching `remove`. I’ll treat that as a hypothesis. The key verification is whether the checkout’s DFS mutates that state in place and whether sibling branches reuse it.

items.text: Inspect allocator/backtracking and existing shared test structure
Add a failing public regression case for list-valued matching
Fix branch-local state restoration across allocator variants
Run focused pinned-container tests and review diff

item.text:
The checkout confirms the bug. With list attributes enabled, `add` destructively narrows `intersection`; `remove` only decrements `numDevices`. A failed DFS branch therefore leaks its narrower intersection into the next sibling branch. Scalar matching does not mutate equivalent state, which is why existing scalar backtracking coverage passes.

I’m adding the regression first with four devices: the first branch narrows to `value1` and dead-ends; the valid sibling branch requires restoring `{value1,value2}` so it can select three devices sharing `value2`.

The regression is in place. The requested container cannot currently reach the host Docker socket (`permission denied`), so I can’t record the expected red run here. I’m continuing with the verified state fix and will retry the exact command after formatting; the environment limitation is mechanical, not an allocator mystery for once.

items.text: Inspect allocator/backtracking and existing shared test structure
Add a failing public regression case for list-valued matching
Fix branch-local state restoration across allocator variants
Run focused pinned-container tests and review diff

item.text: The fix now stores one immutable intersection snapshot per successful allocation depth and pops it on `remove`. I verified the set intersection helper returns a new set, so the shallow struct copy does not alias a subsequently mutated map. The remaining question is test execution; I’m checking the DFS ordering against the new case and then doing a final repository sanity pass.

arguments.evidence: Verified in the checked-out experimental allocator: list matching mutates one intersection during DFS add while remove only decrements numDevices. Implemented per-depth LIFO intersection snapshots and a shared public allocation regression. Docker test execution was blocked by permission denied on the host Docker socket.

Verified in the checked-out experimental allocator: list matching mutates one intersection during DFS add while remove only decrements numDevices. Implemented per-depth LIFO intersection snapshots and a shared public allocation regression. Docker test execution was blocked by permission denied on the host Docker socket.

item.text:
The public case’s search order is confirmed from the checkout: it tries devices 1→2, exhausts 3 and 4, removes device 2, then retries at device 3. That is exactly the stale-state transition. The fix restores the prior depth before that retry.

Static review is clean (`git diff --check`). Both the focused regression command and the full requested package command were attempted; Docker denied access to its Unix socket before Go started.

items.text: Inspect allocator/backtracking and existing shared test structure
Add a failing public regression case for list-valued matching
Fix branch-local state restoration across allocator variants
Run focused pinned-container tests and review diff

888	type matchAttributeConstraint struct {
   889		logger        klog.Logger // Includes name and attribute name, so no need to repeat in log messages.
   890		requestNames  sets.Set[string]
   891		attributeName resourceapi.FullyQualifiedName
   892		features      Features
   893	
   894		// For scalar values (existing behavior)
   895		attribute *resourceapi.DeviceAttribute
   896	
   897		// Backtracking removes devices in LIFO order, so retain the intersection
   898		// for each depth of the search.
   899		intersections []*deviceAttributeListAsSet
   900	
   901		numDevices int
   902	}
   903	
   904	func (m *matchAttributeConstraint) add(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID) bool {
   905		if m.requestNames.Len() > 0 && !m.matches(requestName, subRequestName) {
   906			// Device not affected by constraint.
   907			m.logger.V(7).Info("Constraint does not apply to request", "request", requestName)
   908			return true
   909		}
   910	
   911		attribute := lookupAttribute(device, deviceID, m.attributeName)
   912		if attribute == nil {
   913			// Doesn't have the attribute.
   914			m.logger.V(7).Info("Constraint not satisfied, attribute not set")
   915			return false
   916		}
   917	
   918		if m.numDevices == 0 {
   919			// The first device can always get picked.
   920			// Initialize either scalar attribute or list set based on the attribute type.
   921			if m.features.ListTypeAttributes {
   922				// Convert attribute to set representation (both scalar and list)
   923				intersection := attributeAsSet(attribute)
   924				if intersection == nil {
   925					m.logger.V(7).Info("Attribute type unknown")
   926					return false
   927				}
   928				m.intersections = append(m.intersections, intersection)
   929			} else {
   930				// Scalar attribute: use existing behavior
   931				m.attribute = attribute
   932			}
   933			m.numDevices = 1
   934			m.logger.V(7).Info("First in set")
   935			return true
   936		}
   937	
   938		// Check if we are matching with set-based logic or scalar-based logic
   939		if m.features.ListTypeAttributes {
   940			// Set-based matching: check if there is non-empty intersection
   941			newSet := attributeAsSet(attribute)
   942			if newSet == nil {
   943				m.logger.V(7).Info("Unknown attribute type")
   944				return false
   945			}
   946			intersection := m.intersections[len(m.intersections)-1]
   947			if !intersection.hasIntersection(newSet) {
   948				m.logger.V(7).Info("Attribute values have no common elements")
   949				return false
   950			}
   951			// Preserve the prior intersection for a matching remove during backtracking.
   952			nextIntersection := *intersection
   953			nextIntersection.updateToIntersection(newSet)
   954			m.intersections = append(m.intersections, &nextIntersection)
   955		} else {
   956			// Scalar matching: use existing behavior
   957			switch {
   958			case attribute.StringValue != nil:
   959				if m.attribute.StringValue == nil || *attribute.StringValue != *m.attribute.StringValue {
   960					m.logger.V(7).Info("String values different")
   961					return false
   962				}
   963			case attribute.IntValue != nil:
   964				if m.attribute.IntValue == nil || *attribute.IntValue != *m.attribute.IntValue {
   965					m.logger.V(7).Info("Int values different")
   966					return false
   967				}
   968			case attribute.BoolValue != nil:
   969				if m.attribute.BoolValue == nil || *attribute.BoolValue != *m.attribute.BoolValue {
   970					m.logger.V(7).Info("Bool values different")
   971					return false
   972				}
   973			case attribute.VersionValue != nil:
   974				// semver 2.0.0 requires that version strings are in their
   975				// minimal form (in particular, no leading zeros). Therefore a
   976				// strict "exact equal" check can do a string comparison.
   977				if m.attribute.VersionValue == nil || *attribute.VersionValue != *m.attribute.VersionValue {
   978					m.logger.V(7).Info("Version values different")
   979					return false
   980				}
   981			default:
   982				// Unknown value type, cannot match.
   983				m.logger.V(7).Info("Unknown attribute type")
   984				return false
   985			}
   986		}
   987	
   988		m.numDevices++
   989		m.logger.V(7).Info("Constraint satisfied by device", "device", deviceID, "numDevices", m.numDevices)
   990		return true
   991	}
   992	
   993	func (m *matchAttributeConstraint) remove(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID) {
   994		if m.requestNames.Len() > 0 && !m.matches(requestName, subRequestName) {
   995			// Device not affected by constraint.
   996			return
   997		}
   998	
   999		m.numDevices--
  1000		if m.features.ListTypeAttributes {
  1001			m.intersections = m.intersections[:len(m.intersections)-1]
  1002		}
  1003		m.logger.V(7).Info("Device removed from constraint set", "device", deviceID, "numDevices", m.numDevices)
  1004	}

item.text:
Implemented the allocator fix and regression coverage.

- Root cause: list-valued match intersections were narrowed during DFS but not restored during backtracking.
- Fix: retain an intersection snapshot per allocation depth and pop it during `remove`, covering every supported attribute type.
- Regression: shared allocator test forces `{device1, device2}` to dead-end, then verifies backtracking finds `{device1, device3, device4}`.

- [allocator_experimental.go](/Users/ericlu/Works/openAI-hackathon/benchmarks/kub1/trials/target-informed-01/worktree/snapshot-v1/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/experimental/allocator_experimental.go:888)
- [allocator_testing.go](/Users/ericlu/Works/openAI-hackathon/benchmarks/kub1/trials/target-informed-01/worktree/snapshot-v1/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/allocatortesting/allocator_testing.go:6054)

`git diff --check` passes. Both the focused regression test and full requested package test were attempted, but Docker denied access to its socket before the container started:

```text
permission denied while trying to connect to the docker API
```
